title: alsine kotschyana
intro: Alsine kotschyana, also known as the Mountain Sorrel or Alpine Sorrel, is a perennial herb belonging to the saxifrage family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to treat digestive issues, kidney problems, and as a diuretic. The plant is rich in vitamins and minerals, offering several health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as an antioxidant. Herbal preparations of Alsine kotschyana include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other topical applications.
